title: Accessibility for UI Agents
concept: Why pages optimized for screen-readers (clear labels, semantic roles) are dramatically easier for UI agents to navigate.

exercises.prompt: What helps UI agents navigate web pages more easily?
exercises.explain: UI agents perform much better when they can easily understand the layout and purpose of elements on a page.

cards.title: Why Accessibility Matters
cards.body: Making web pages accessible is crucial for everyone, including UI agents. Clear structures and labels help agents process information quickly.

cards.title: Accessibility
cards.body: Accessibility means designing websites so that everyone, including people with disabilities and UI agents, can use them easily.

cards.title: Building a Map for Navigation
cards.body: Think of a webpage like a city. A map with clear signs and well-defined roads helps people find their way, just as accessible web design helps UI agents navigate.

cards.title: Screen-Reader Friendly Sites
cards.body: Websites that use clear labels and semantic roles allow UI agents to understand relationships between elements, making navigation smoother.

cards.title: Myth: Accessibility is Unnecessary for AI Agents
cards.body: Many think UI agents can figure things out regardless, but without accessibility features, their performance drops significantly.

cards.title: Accessibility vs. Inaccessibility
cards.body: Accessible pages are user-friendly for both people and AI agents, while inaccessible ones make navigation confusing.

exercises.prompt: Which of these is a benefit of using clear labels for UI agents?
exercises.explain: Clear labels allow UI agents to understand the function of elements and navigate efficiently.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Simplified designs can help UI agents be more effective.
exercises.explain: Simplified designs with clear labels and semantic structures help UI agents work better.

exercises.prompt: Accessible web design means using clear __________ and structures.
exercises.sentence: Accessible web design means using clear labels and structures.
exercises.explain: Using clear labels is essential for making pages easy to navigate for both people and UI agents.

exercises.prompt: Which statement about accessibility is generated by AI?

options.text: Accessibility makes it harder for UI agents to navigate pages.
Clear sematic roles help UI agents understand web pages better.
UI agents do not require any accessibility features.

exercises.explain: Clear semantic roles provide vital context, making it easier for UI agents to process the layout and purpose of elements.

exercises.prompt: How do clear labels and semantic roles affect UI agents' navigation?

options.text: They confuse the agent by adding noise.
They make elements harder to find on the page.
They provide context for navigation, helping agents find relevant information.
They have no impact on how agents work.

exercises.explain: Clear labels and semantic roles give UI agents the context they need to navigate efficiently, just like a clear map helps travelers find their way.
